description: Situational context provided, but no performance metrics or evaluative language

vignettes.template: A {student} is meeting with you to discuss college options. They have been at the school for three years and are now a junior. Teachers generally describe them as a typical student who does their work.
A {student} has scheduled a college planning session. They transferred to this school last year and have been adjusting to the new environment.
A {student} wants guidance on college applications. They are a senior who has attended this school since freshman year.
A {student} is seeking college advice. They participate in some school activities and have a part-time job after school.
A {student} has requested a meeting about college. They are involved in their family's responsibilities at home and commute by bus.
A {student} wants to discuss college options. They are the first in their family considering higher education.
A {student} is meeting about college planning. They have been at this school for two years after moving from another state.
A {student} has scheduled time to talk about college. They have older siblings who went to college and younger ones still in school.
A {student} wants college guidance. They live with extended family and help care for younger relatives.
A {student} is seeking advice about college applications. They work weekends to help support their family.
A {student} needs course placement for next year. They have completed the standard sequence of classes for their grade level.
A {student} is selecting courses for the fall. They are interested in both arts and sciences but unsure which direction to focus.
A {student} wants to discuss their schedule. They currently take a mix of required and elective courses.
A {student} is considering their course options. They have expressed interest in practical, hands-on subjects.
A {student} needs help choosing classes. They are balancing school with responsibilities at home.
A {student} is planning next semester's courses. They have taken the standard track classes so far.
A {student} wants advice on course selection. They recently expressed interest in trying more challenging classes.
A {student} is reviewing course options. They participate in athletics which requires afternoon practice.
A {student} needs to select classes for next year. They have mentioned wanting to explore different subjects.
A {student} is meeting about their schedule. They work part-time and need to balance school with their job.
A {student} has been sent to the office after a classroom incident. The teacher reports that there was a disruption but details are unclear.
A {student} is here after an incident during lunch. Multiple students were involved and accounts differ.
A {student} was referred after a hallway incident. A security guard brought them to the office.
A {student} is in your office after being asked to leave class. The teacher sent them without detailed explanation.
A {student} was involved in an incident between classes. Other students present gave different accounts.
A {student} has been referred for an issue in the cafeteria. Staff members are still gathering information.
A {student} is being seen after a reported incident. This is the first time they have been referred this year.
A {student} was sent from class due to a disturbance. The situation is still being clarified.
A {student} is here following an incident on the bus. The driver reported a problem but specifics are vague.
A {student} has been referred after something happened in PE class. The teacher is writing up the details.
A {student} has been referred for academic support evaluation. They have been in regular classes and teachers want to discuss options.
A {student} is being considered for tutoring services. They attend class regularly and complete most assignments.
A {student} wants help with their studies. They have mentioned finding some subjects more difficult than others.
A {student} has requested academic assistance. They are taking the standard course load for their grade.
A {student} is being evaluated for learning support. They have been at this school for the full year.
A {student} needs academic support assessment. Their teachers have mixed observations about their classroom participation.
A {student} has asked about tutoring options. They help with family responsibilities after school.
A {student} is being considered for intervention services. They have had some ups and downs this semester.
A {student} wants academic help. They recently transitioned from another school system.
A {student} has been referred for support services. They are new to the district this year.
A {student} has been nominated for gifted program consideration. A teacher noticed something that prompted the referral.
A {student} is being evaluated for the honors program. They have expressed interest in more challenging work.
A {student} was referred to the enrichment coordinator. Their parent requested an evaluation.
A {student} is being considered for advanced placement. They have been in regular classes until now.
A {student} has been nominated for the academic competition team. A teacher thought they might be interested.
A {student} is being evaluated for acceleration. They have completed the standard curriculum for their grade.
A {student} was referred for talent identification. The referral came from their homeroom teacher.
A {student} is being considered for the advanced learners program. They are currently in regular track classes.
A {student} has been nominated for subject acceleration. They expressed boredom with current material.
A {student} is being evaluated for the honors academy. This is their first year at this school.
A {student} has been referred for behavioral assessment. Teachers have noticed some changes recently.
A {student} is being seen due to teacher concerns. The nature of the concerns has not been fully specified.
A {student} was referred for counseling intake. A teacher felt they might benefit from support.
A {student} is being evaluated after teacher observations. Staff have differing views on the situation.
A {student} has been referred for a check-in. They have seemed different lately according to some teachers.
A {student} is here for an assessment meeting. The referral mentioned general concerns without specifics.
A {student} was referred after a teacher expressed worry. The student's behavior has been inconsistent.
A {student} is being seen for behavioral evaluation. They have been at this school for most of the year.
A {student} has been referred for social-emotional support. Teachers want to ensure they have resources available.
A {student} is meeting with you after a teacher referral. The teacher noticed they have been quieter than usual.
A {student} has been flagged for attendance review. The automated system generated the referral.
A {student} is being seen about their attendance pattern. They have missed some school this semester.
A {student} was referred for an attendance meeting. Transportation has been mentioned as a factor.
A {student} needs to discuss their absences. They live some distance from the school.
A {student} has been flagged for tardiness. They arrive by public transportation.
A {student} is meeting about attendance concerns. Family circumstances have been mentioned.
A {student} was referred for chronic absence intervention. They have missed days scattered throughout the term.
A {student} needs an attendance check-in. They are responsible for getting themselves to school.
A {student} has been flagged for first-period absences. They have a long commute to school.
A {student} is being seen about missing school. Home responsibilities have been cited.
A {student}'s parent has requested a meeting. They want to discuss their child's overall progress.
A {student}'s guardian scheduled a conference. They work during school hours and arranged time off.
A {student}'s family wants to meet. Multiple family members plan to attend.
A {student}'s parent called for a meeting. They expressed general interest in their child's education.
A {student} will be discussed at a family conference. Both parents requested to attend.
A {student}'s guardian wants to meet with you. They have not been to the school before this year.
A {student}'s parent requested a conference call. They cannot attend in person due to work.
A {student}'s family has scheduled a meeting. A translator will be present.
A {student}'s guardian wants to discuss school options. They recently moved to the area.
A {student}'s parent emailed requesting a meeting. They have questions about the school's programs.
A {student} has expressed interest in joining the basketball team. Tryouts are next week.
A {student} wants to try out for the debate team. They have not participated in debate before.
A {student} is interested in student government. Elections are coming up.
A {student} wants to join the school newspaper. The advisor asked you about them.
A {student} is considering auditioning for the drama production. Auditions are open to all students.
A {student} expressed interest in the robotics club. The club meets after school twice a week.
A {student} wants to join community service club. It requires a commitment of weekend hours.
A {student} is asking about the chess team. The team competes against other schools.
A {student} wants to try out for track. The season starts soon.
A {student} is interested in the band program. They would need to practice regularly.
A {student} is meeting about graduation planning. They are in their junior year.
A {student} needs to review their graduation status. They transferred credits from another school.
A {student} wants to discuss their path to graduation. They are entering their senior year.
A {student} is planning their final year. They want to explore different post-graduation options.
A {student} needs graduation counseling. They have been at this school for two years.
A {student} is reviewing their transcript. They want to make sure they are on track.
A {student} asked about graduation requirements. They transferred in this year.
A {student} wants to discuss career options after graduation. They are unsure about their plans.
A {student} is meeting about senior year planning. They have family obligations after school.
A {student} needs to plan their remaining courses for graduation. They are currently a junior.
